symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced bowel frequency over ~6 days with small, hard, pellet-like stools

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Infrequent, hard, lumpy stools (Bristol type 1-2) are a core Rome IV criterion for functional constipation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Straining with sense of incomplete evacuation and use of digital perineal splinting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Straining, incomplete evacuation and manual manoeuvres are Rome IV criteria and point to an evacuation/dyssynergic component.

symptoms.symptom_name: Colicky lower-abdominal bloating relieved transiently by passing flatus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distension from faecal loading that eases with flatus fits a loaded but patent colon.
Relief with passing flatus indicates the lumen is not occluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Continued passage of flatus with no vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absolute constipation (no flatus) and bilious vomiting are hallmarks of obstruction; their absence strongly argues against it.
A patent colon still passing gas is consistent with functional loading rather than blockage.

symptoms.symptom_name: No rectal bleeding and no unintentional weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bleeding and weight loss lowers the pre-test probability of malignancy, though it does not remove the need for routine age-appropriate screening.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-tender abdomen with palpable firm faecal loading in the left lower quadrant and active bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable descending-colon faecal loading with a soft non-tender abdomen and active (non-obstructive) bowel sounds — sealed clinician finding; the AI Doctor infers loading from history, not palpation.
No distension with tinkling/absent bowel sounds and no peritonism argue against obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Digital rectal examination: hard inspissated stool in the vault, normal sphincter tone, no rigid impaction, no mass, no blood on the glove

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stool in the vault without a solid immovable fecalith argues against true impaction with overflow — sealed clinician finding not obtainable in telehealth.
No palpable rectal mass and no blood on the glove; a normal DRE does not exclude a proximal lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Plain abdominal radiograph showing isolated faecal loading without bowel dilatation or obstruction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Faecal loading without dilated bowel loops would confirm the absence of obstruction — an outpatient imaging test, never available in real time in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Benign functional constipation with patent colon
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Hard infrequent stools with straining and incomplete evacuation, plus preserved flatus and no vomiting, together identify a loaded but non-obstructed colon amenable to conservative management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Age-appropriate bowel-cancer screening (e.g. faecal occult blood test) arranged routinely via GP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A persistent change in bowel habit warrants routine structural screening even when acute alarm features are absent.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods to exclude secondary causes (thyroid function, calcium, glucose) if constipation is recurrent
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Hypothyroidism, hypercalcaemia and diabetes are reversible contributors to chronic constipation and are checked in the non-urgent GP work-up.
Not indicated in a stable patient passing flatus with no vomiting and no peritonism; ordering it would reflect over-triage. Reserved for if obstruction red flags develop.
